title: wrightia coraia
intro: Wrightia coraia, also known as Coraia or Indian snake root, is a plant species in the Apocynaceae family, native to India and Southeast Asia. The plant is used in traditional medicine for its various medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiseptic properties, which are attributed to the presence of alkaloids and glycosides. The health benefits of Wrightia coraia include its ability to treat fever, digestive issues, and respiratory problems, as well as its use in the treatment of snake bites. Traditional herbal preparations include the plant's leaves, roots, and bark, which can be made into teas, decoctions, or infusions to harness its medicinal properties.
